llm_response:
plastic pollution harms marine ecosystems by trapping animals in debris and causing them to ingest harmful materials. tiny pieces of plastic known as microplastics enter the water and are mistaken for food by many sea creatures. this ingestion leads to internal injuries and starvation, which weakens populations across the globe.

the presence of plastic reduces biodiversity in marine ecosystems by destroying habitats like coral reefs and seagrass beds. when animals cannot find safe places to live or feed, the balance of the ocean shifts dramatically. many species struggle to survive because their natural environments are filled with foreign waste instead of clean water.

plastic also disrupts the food chain as larger animals eat contaminated prey and pass toxins to predators higher up. this contamination spreads through marine ecosystems, affecting everything from small fish to large whales. ultimately, the health of the entire ocean depends on reducing the amount of plastic that enters our waters.
